channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Jazz losing skid hits eight after 126-118 loss to Toronto
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Scottie Barnes had 20 points and 10 rebounds, Immanuel Quickley also scored 20 points and the Toronto Raptors beat the Utah Jazz 126-118 on Friday night. A.J. Lawson added 18 points, RJ Barrett had 17 and Orlando Robinson finished with 14 points, 10 rebounds and five assists.
item.dc:creator: ABC 4

item.title: Plucky Lakers nearly beat Nuggets despite Luka Doncic, LeBron James among LA's sidelined starters
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Luka Doncic sat out the Los Angeles Lakers' 131-126 loss at Denver on Friday night, cheering on his scrappy, short-handed teammates who nearly pulled off a shocker without four starters.
item.dc:creator: ARNIE STAPLETON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zuby Ejiofor much more than just most improved in record-setting performance for No. 6 St. John's
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Maybe most improved didn't really do Zuby Ejiofor justice. The junior forward delivered an overpowering performance Friday night for No. 6 St.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ejiofor powers No. 6 St. John's past No. 25 Marquette 79-63 into 1st Big East final since 2000
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Zuby Ejiofor scored a career-high 33 points, a St. John's record in the Big East Tournament, and the sixth-ranked Red Storm pulled away from No. 25 Marquette 79-63 on Friday night to reach the conference championship game for the first time in 25 years.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: No. 96 fits Hurricanes' Roslovic just right as he scores goal 2nd straight game
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Jack Roslovic got his No. 96 jersey back, and he's wearing it well. The Carolina Hurricanes center scored a goal in a second straight game and he has four points over three games since reclaiming the number.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Withers' lane violation adds improbable twist to UNC's failed comeback against No. 1 Duke in ACCs
item.description: North Carolina graduate forward Jae'Lyn Withers committed a lane violation with 4.1 seconds left that negated a tying free throw in the Tar Heels' loss to No. 1 Duke in the Atlantic Coast Conference Tournament.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Beard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: No. 1 Duke holds off furious North Carolina rally to win 74-71, reach ACC title game without Flagg
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Kon Knueppel scored 17 points and No. 1 Duke held off a furious second half rally by North Carolina to beat its rival for the third time this season, 74-71 on Friday night to reach the Atlantic Coast Conference Tournament championship game.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Jessie Holmes says his dogs deserve all the credit for his 'magical' first Iditarod win
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Bundled-up well wishers lined a street along the Bering Sea coastline in the early morning darkness Friday, cheering musher Jessie Holmes as he won Alaska's Iditarod Trail Sled Dog Race.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Min Woo Lee and Akshay Bhatia share lead at The Players as Justin Thomas ties course mark with 62
item.description: PONTE VEDRA BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Min Woo Lee and Akshay Bhatia shared the lead at The Players Championship going into a weekend with strong wind in the forecast and the potential for a wide-open chase. That includes Justin Thomas, the biggest surprise Friday.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Female athletes to testify against NCAA, demanding sex screenings to keep trans athletes out of women's sports
item.description: A group of women college athletes affected by transgender inclusion will testify in a legal battle between the NCAA and the state of Texas, demanding sex screenings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cooper Flagg and Maliq Brown miss No. 1 Duke's win against rival UNC in ACC semifinals
item.description: Top-ranked Duke played without Cooper Flagg and Maliq Brown in its win against rival North Carolina in the Atlantic Conference Tournament because of injuries.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Beard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Maine's federal funding freeze from Trump's USDA reversed
item.description: The federal funding freeze against Maine imposed by President Donald Trump's Department of Agriculture during Title IX and Title VI investigations has been reversed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michigan State basketball surges past Oregon to secure Big Ten Tournament semi-final spot
item.description: The Big Ten Tournament’s No. 1 seeded Michigan State Spartans outmanned the No. 8 seeded Oregon Ducks 74-64 to advance to the semi-finals.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Carr, Local 4

item.title: Heartbreak for DePaul as 1st trip to Big East semifinals slips away with late collapse to Creighton
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — At long last, downtrodden DePaul was finally about to produce its own indelible moment at the Big East Tournament. A big upset was in the bag for the Blue Demons — and their first trip to the semifinals at Madison Square Garden. And then it slipped through their fingers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Teen track runner who bashed opponent's head rallies supporters in Virginia after getting assault charge
item.description: Virginia teen Alaila Everett held a rally outside of her high school Thursday, as supporters showed sympathy amid national backlash.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Plateau Valley basketball advances to 2A Final Four
item.description: In head coach Mike Vig's final season, the Cowboys find themselves just two wins away from a State Championship.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Hepburn's buzzer-beater lifts No. 13 Louisville to another big moment in Kelsey-led resurgence
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Chucky Hepburn had given up the ball late in a tie game to a teammate, and it looked like the next time he might touch the ball again would come in overtime.
item.dc:creator: AARON BEARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chiefs tap Gardner Minshew as Patrick Mahomes' new backup for 2025 season: reports
item.description: Gardner Minshew's newest home in the NFL is the Kansas City Chiefs, who signed him to a one-year deal to be Patrick Mahomes' backup for the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rory McIlroy mum on video of him taking phone from University of Texas player heckling him
item.description: PONTE VEDRA BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Rory McIlroy was interested only in his good start to The Players Championship on Thursday than discussing a heckling incident in which he took the phone of a University of Texas player who had heckled him during a practice round.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: John Feinstein, bestselling author and Washington Post sportswriter, dead at 68
item.description: John Feinstein, a bestselling author of 50 books and a longtime Washington Post sports writer and columnist, died at the age of 68 Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jazz fined $100,000 for not playing Markkanen
item.description: MEMPHIS (ABC4 Sports) - The NBA on Wednesday fined the Utah Jazz $100,000 for violating its player participation policy.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Warriors star Stephen Curry reaches 25,000 career points
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Stephen Curry became the 26th player in NBA history to score 25,000 career points, reaching the milestone during the third quarter of the Golden State Warriors' 115-110 victory over the Detroit Pistons on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: RSL gives up two stoppage time goals in 3-1 loss
item.description: SANDY, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - After jumping out to an early lead, Real Salt Lake allowed three straight goals, two in stoppage time, and loses in its first meeting with San Diego FC Saturday night at America First Field, 3-1.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Utah Valley clinches WAC regular season title
item.description: STEPHENVILLE, Tex. (ABC4 Sports) - Dominick Nelson matched a career high with 25 points, and Carter Welling added 18 as Utah Valley defeated Tarleton State 71-66 in a sold-out Wisdom Gym on Saturday night, closing out WAC play with a statement win.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Amber Whiting resigns as BYU women's basketball coach
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - BYU women's basketball coach Amber Whiting announced she is resigning after three seasons in Provo. The decision to part ways was mutual between Whiting and the school. Assistant coach Lee Cummard will serve as the interim head coach for now.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Real Salt Lake aims to keep winning as San Diego FC visits for the first time
item.description: Real Salt Lake returns to action at America First Field on Saturday night, facing MLS expansion side San Diego FC for the first time. Coming off a commanding 2-0 win over Seattle last weekend, RSL looks to build momentum and keep their shutout streak alive in front of their raucous home crowd.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Casey, ABC 4

item.title: Trump will lead task force preparing for 2026 World Cup
item.description: President Donald Trump has created a task force to prepare for the 2026 World Cup, which will bring the globe's premier soccer tournament to North America at a time when his on-again, off-again tariffs have ratcheted up tensions across the continent.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Blackhawks in action against the Predators following overtime victory
item.description: Chicago Blackhawks (20-35-8, in the Central Division) vs. Nashville Predators (23-32-7, in the Central Division) Nashville, Tennessee; Saturday, 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chicago visits Miami after White's 44-point game
item.description: Chicago Bulls (25-38, 10th in the Eastern Conference) vs. Miami Heat (29-33, seventh in the Eastern Conference) Miami; Saturday, 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
